div.head: The Secretary of State to the Secretary of the Navy (Forrestal)
div.p: My Dear Mr. Secretary: Thank you for your letter of February 27, 1947, in regard our sending a mission to China composed of men with practical knowledge in the field of government, finance, banking, production, transportation and agriculture.

I am in full accord with the idea and hope that conditions in China may soon make it worthwhile to put it into practice. However, until we are in a position to extend substantial credits to the Chinese I do not think it would be realistic to send such a mission. In this connection your attention is invited to the final paragraph of the enclosed
copy of my letter Supra. in reply to a recent letter from Secretary Patterson,Dated February 26, p. 790. copy of which is also enclosed.
